name: Pina-Colada Cake
recipeInstructions:
1. Allow all ingredients to come to room temperature.
2. Preheat the oven to 350°F and grease an 8-inch or 9-inch cake pan.
3. In a blender, combine the first five ingredients until smooth. Pour into a mixing bowl and sift the next three ingredients on top of the blended mixture. Beat until all lumps are gone, but do not overmix.
4. Fold in the pineapple and pour the batter into the greased cake pan. Bake for 40 to 45 minutes, or until a toothpick inserted in the center comes out clean.
5. Remove from the oven and let cool in the pan for about 15 minutes before turning onto a cooling rack.
6. Sprinkle shredded coconut over the entire surface of the warm cake and allow it to cool completely.
While the cake is baking:
1. In a saucepan, mix coconut milk and arrowroot until the arrowroot dissolves. Heat on low, stirring constantly until the mixture boils and thickens. Add the pineapple juice concentrate and stir in, continuing to boil and thicken. Remove from heat and let cool.
2. Repeat the process with crushed pineapple and arrowroot in a separate saucepan.
Once everything is cooled:
1. Split the cake into two layers. Spread the thickened coconut milk over the entire surface of the cake like icing.
2. Sprinkle shredded coconut over the entire surface and refrigerate until serving time.
